title: Xenial Savory Norwegian Fish Stew
description: A hearty and savory Norwegian fish stew made with double cream and a variety of seafood, perfect for a cold winter's night. This stew is also ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly and can be prepared in under an hour.
instructions: In a large pot, combine the chopped onion, carrots, and potatoes, and saute over medium heat until the vegetables are tender.
Add the cod and salmon fillets to the pot, and cook until the fish is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
Pour in the double cream, water, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the stew to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and let cook for 10 minutes.
Use a spoon to gently fold the stew, being careful not to break up the fish.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Let the stew rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed before serving.
